decisions.decision: Cinematic video hero (Velorah-style) at /, lab-notebook-then-video-ASCII at /v1, cinematic mirror parked at /v2
decisions.rationale: user picked 'Cinematic video + researcher Lottie below' from option list; iterated through ASCII concepts (vertical streams → terminal feed → lab notebook → live video-ASCII) before landing on the video-ASCII look at /v1

decisions.decision: Single LandingObservers client component owns ALL global behaviors (IntersectionObserver, scroll listener, JS smooth-scroll, scroll parallax progress var)
decisions.rationale: minimizes 'use client' boundaries; sections stay server-rendered + enhanced by one client orchestrator

decisions.decision: JS smooth-scroll instead of CSS scroll-behavior: smooth
decisions.rationale: CSS version respects prefers-reduced-motion which most macOS users have on by default; JS version always smooths regardless

decisions.decision: Live in-browser video → ASCII conversion on /v1 (no preprocessing pipeline, no ffmpeg)
decisions.rationale: Canvas getImageData + Rec.709 luminance + character density ramp at 24 FPS works entirely in the browser; ships zero new deps

decisions.decision: Static SVG file for posterize filter (public/cine-filter.svg) instead of inline JSX <svg>
decisions.rationale: Safari hydration mismatch when SVG rendered with width=0/height=0 and React tree expects matching client output; static asset removes SVG from React tree

decisions.decision: No new npm deps (Framer Motion, Lottie, etc. NOT added)
decisions.rationale: project CLAUDE.md hard rule #5; CSS animations + Canvas API + IntersectionObserver cover everything we needed without bundle bloat

uncommitted_files.note: Modified by parallel Phase 2 chat — landing-polish chat must not commit this file
